rationale: Koenigstorfer and Baumgartner (2016) is cited multiple times in the theoretical framing and contribution discussion, not just as a passing reference. The citing paper uses it as evidence for a key premise that restrained eaters respond differently to food cues and are generally more interested in lower-calorie options. The 2020 study then positions its own contribution as moving beyond that prior work by comparing two calorie-equated lower-calorie routes (lower caloric density vs smaller portion size).
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as prior evidence on restrained eaters' responses to food labeling/calorie-related cues, then builds on that stream by testing a new trade-off between two lower-calorie option formats. In this sense, it extends the earlier line of work to a different decision structure.
